page: Microsoft Azure D128als_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dalsv7 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 128 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 64 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16, and SHA_NI. The server is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, though it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark data places this instance in the top tier for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with cached small-block read speeds reaching 12,078.81 GB/sec and uncached large-block write speeds at 199.81 GB/sec. Cryptographic and integer operations are supported by hardware-accelerated instructions, resulting in top-tier Passmark CPU encryption and database operation scores. In network and application-level tests, the server achieved 173.2 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and sustained a static web serving throughput of 99.09 GB/sec. Large language model inference benchmarks show strong to top-tier performance, processing 310.23 tokens per second on a medium 7B model.

The primary tradeoff of the D128als_v7 is its low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core and the complete absence of local storage. This configuration makes it highly cost-efficient for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive in-memory databases or local NVMe scratch space. It is designed for workloads such as high-throughput web serving, CPU-based machine learning inference, intensive cryptographic processing, and multi-threaded computational tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D128als_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by a 128-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Featuring 256.0 GB of RAM, this instance provides a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, prioritizing raw compute density over memory capacity. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs but supports premium remote storage. Benchmark results place the server in the top 10% for single-core and multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and cryptographic execution. It demonstrates exceptional throughput in Redis operations and static web serving. The D128als_v7 is engineered for compute-intensive workloads, including high-concurrency web serving, CPU-based machine learning inference, and multi-threaded data processing pipelines.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D128als_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 128 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor, 256 GB of RAM, and top-tier CPU and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D128als_v7 is a 128-vCPU AMD EPYC virtual machine with 256 GB RAM, optimized for high-compute workloads and top-tier processing.
tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring 128 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and a streamlined memory profile for intensive parallel processing.
bullet_points: Powered by a 128-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz
Equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM providing 2.0 GB per vCPU
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and cryptographic performance
No bundled local storage or dedicated GPU hardware
Best suited for high-throughput web serving and CPU-based inference
